ucs.title: [LLM] Rancor (G0075) DDKONG/PLAINTEE installer: Office/WScript spawning msiexec to fetch remote MSI
ucs.description: Hunts Rancor's signature delivery chain where a politically-themed lure document (typically targeting ASEAN/SE-Asia government and diplomatic entities) drops a VBScript that invokes msiexec /q /i against a remote HTTP(S) URL to stage DDKONG or PLAINTEE. The Office/wscript-->msiexec-->external-URL combination is a near-fingerprint of Rancor's 2018+ campaigns documented by Unit 42.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.process_id) as pid from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=msiexec.exe (Processes.parent_process_name IN (winword.exe,excel.exe,powerpnt.exe,outlook.exe,wscript.exe,cscript.exe,mshta.exe,cmd.exe)) (Processes.process="*http://*" OR Processes.process="*https://*" OR Processes.process="*\\\\*") (Processes.process="* /q*" OR Processes.process="* /quiet*" OR Processes.process="* /i *") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | join type=left process_guid [| tstats summariesonly=true values(All_Traffic.dest) as remote_dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app=msiexec.exe by All_Traffic.process_guid | rename All_Traffic.process_guid as process_guid] | where isnotnull(remote_dest)
ucs.kql:
let suspiciousParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName =~ "msiexec.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName has_any (suspiciousParents)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","\\\\")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/q","/quiet","/i ")
| extend Chain = strcat(InitiatingProcessParentFileName, " -> ", InitiatingProcessFileName, " -> ", FileName)
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "msiexec.exe"
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (".asia",".ph",".kh",".sg",".my",".id",".vn",".th") or RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | summarize RemoteUrls=make_set(RemoteUrl,10), RemoteIPs=make_set(RemoteIP,10) by DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId, InitiatingProcessCreationTime
) on DeviceId, $left.ProcessId == $right.InitiatingProcessId
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, Chain, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, RemoteUrls, RemoteIPs, ReportId
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Rancor's Unit 42-documented chain (the Cambodia/Singapore lures) is uniquely characterised by Office or Windows Script Host invoking msiexec with /q /i and a remote HTTP path to stage the DDKONG loader and PLAINTEE backdoor. Legitimate enterprise software almost never uses msiexec to silently fetch an MSI from an external URL via a script host or Office child, making the parent-child + URL combina

ucs.title: [LLM] Rancor PLAINTEE post-install persistence: schtasks + Reg Run + WMI subscription writing C2 over HTTP
ucs.description: Hunts Rancor's PLAINTEE post-compromise persistence pattern, where the implant registers itself via schtasks /create or a Run-key while beaconing to hard-coded C2 over HTTP/UDP. The combination of scheduled-task or AccessibilityFeatures-style debugger persistence created by a non-standard binary in %APPDATA% / %TEMP%, followed by outbound HTTP from that same binary, mirrors the PLAINTEE behaviour observed in Rancor's SE-Asia operations.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_path) as path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=schtasks.exe AND Processes.process="*/create*" AND (Processes.process="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\ProgramData\\*")) OR (Processes.process_name=wmic.exe AND Processes.process="*__EventFilter*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_guid Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true values(All_Traffic.dest) as c2_dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as c2_port values(All_Traffic.app) as c2_app from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.app="*\\AppData\\*" OR All_Traffic.app="*\\Temp\\*" OR All_Traffic.app="*\\ProgramData\\*") (All_Traffic.dest_port IN (80,443,8080,53)) NOT All_Traffic.dest_category=internal by All_Traffic.src | rename All_Traffic.src as dest] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let userDirs = dynamic(["\\AppData\\","\\Temp\\","\\ProgramData\\","\\Public\\"]);
let persist =
  DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
  | where (FileName =~ "schtasks.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "/create" and ProcessCommandLine has_any (userDirs))
     or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "__EventFilter")
     or (FileName =~ "reg.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("\\CurrentVersion\\Run","Image File Execution Options") and ProcessCommandLine has_any (userDirs))
  | project DeviceId, DeviceName, persistTime=Timestamp, persistCmd=ProcessCommandLine, persistParent=InitiatingProcessFileName, persistImage=InitiatingProcessFolderPath;
let beacon =
  DeviceNetworkEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
  | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
  | where RemotePort in (80, 443, 8080, 53)
  | where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (userDirs)
  | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","OneDrive.exe","Teams.exe")
  | summarize beaconCount=count(), beaconHosts=dcount(RemoteIP), firstBeacon=min(Timestamp), lastBeacon=max(Timestamp), c2=make_set(RemoteUrl, 5), ips=make_set(RemoteIP, 5) by DeviceId, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath
  | where beaconCount > 5;
persist
| join kind=inner beacon on DeviceId
| project DeviceName, persistTime, persistCmd, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, beaconCount, c2, ips, firstBeacon, lastBeacon
| order by persistTime desc
ucs.rationale: Pairing persistence creation (schtasks/WMI subscription/Run-key) sourced from a user-writable directory with subsequent web-protocol beaconing from the same staged binary models the documented PLAINTEE implant lifecycle Rancor uses against SE-Asian government targets, rather than alerting on either persistence or C2 alone.
